mappings.rationale: Directly addresses group structure of society including families, social stratification, deviance, and welfare (outline A-D).
Explores group dynamics, prejudice, intergroup relations, and patterns of group behaviour (outline A and B).
Examines the modern family's legal organization including marriage, children's rights, and domestic violence (outline A.2).
Directly addresses the social effects of racial and ethnic prejudice (outline B).
Examines minorities and ethnic groups and their integration within diverse societies (outline A.4).
Antisemitism represents a specific form of ethnic prejudice with profound social effects on a minority group (outline B).
Segregated communities illustrate a form of minority group experience shaped by prejudice (outline A.4 and B).
Diaspora communities function as minorities and ethnic groups within broader societies (outline A.4).
Immigrant groups form minorities within societies, raising questions of social integration (outline A.4).
Refugees represent a special social group requiring disaster relief and community welfare services (outline D.2.e).
The African American experience illustrates the dynamics of a major ethnic minority group under racial prejudice (outline A.4 and B).
The struggle against racial prejudice and for equal social status of minority groups is central to outline B.
Directly addresses adolescence as a special social concern including bullying and delinquency (outline C.1).
Directly addresses old age as a special social concern including family dynamics and institutionalization (outline C.2).
Directly addresses women's social and legal status as a special social concern (outline C.3).
Addresses philosophical dimensions of women's social status including gender oppression and justice (outline C.3).
Traces the historical development of women's social and legal status (outline C.3).
Traces women's social status, suffrage, and rights struggles in the United States (outline C.3).
Directly addresses poverty as a special social concern including family, housing, and income distribution (outline C.5).
Directly addresses criminality and delinquency as special social concerns (outline C.6).
Organized criminality involving extortion and illicit enterprise is a social concern discussed in outline C.6.
Violence as a social phenomenon is related to criminality and delinquency (outline C.6).
Sexual orientation and its social dimensions constitute a special social concern (outline C.7).
Directly addresses drug and alcohol abuse, treatment, and its social and criminal justice dimensions (outline C.9).
The pharmacology of alcohol, cannabis, cocaine, and other substances is relevant to drug abuse (outline C.9).
Directly addresses organized public welfare services, unemployment insurance, and social services (outline D).
Directly addresses social service fields including family welfare, child welfare, community development, and mental health services (outline D.2).
Public health services are relevant to medical social services and community welfare (outline D.2.g).
Psychiatric social services and mental health care serve community and institutional settings (outline D.2.g).
Psychological services are relevant to medical and psychiatric social services (outline D.2.g).
Depression and mental health are conditions requiring psychiatric social services (outline D.2.g).
The historical and social dimensions of mental illness make it a social concern requiring psychiatric services (outline D.2.g).
Dementia is relevant both to old age social concerns and to medical social services (outline C.2 and D.2.g).
Child development and psychology are relevant to child welfare services (outline D.2.b).
The historical treatment of children as a social group is relevant to child and youth welfare (outline D.2.b and D.2.c).
Pandemics are public health crises requiring disaster relief and community welfare responses (outline D.2.e).
HIV/AIDS is both a social and public health concern requiring medical and community social services (outline D.2.g).
The rights framework protects minorities, women, and vulnerable groups from discrimination (outline B and C).
Surveillance technologies and their social effects raise concerns about group monitoring and privacy (outline A and C).
Humanitarian intervention, disaster relief, and organized efforts to alleviate human suffering are discussed in outline D.2.e.
Wollstonecraft's advocacy for women's education and rights is foundational to the modern feminist movement, relevant to C.3 (women in society).
